latest.title: Dogs read human pointing surprisingly well
latest.script_key: dogs can follow a human point fast watch the eyes check the person because dogs are unusually good at using human gestures that skill helps them cooperate with us before any command gets repeated the small glance is social intelligence not random obedience what dog skill should come next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Blue liquid foams because gas is made
latest.script_key: watch this foam grow that blue liquid isn t turning solid it s actually making gas so fast it stretches like a sponge the bubbles push the liquid up because there s nowhere else for the gas to go that s why it looks alive ever wondered what gas looks like when it can t escape

latest.title: Dragonflies lock onto prey in midair
latest.script_key: dragonflies track prey with extreme precision watch the eyes before the flight path because dragonflies process motion fast they can predict where prey is going then adjust in the air like a tiny interceptor that is why the catch looks impossibly clean would you see it coming

latest.title: Seagulls ride mountain winds like skiers
latest.script_key: mountain winds carry seagulls effortlessly no flapping needed watch that wing tip flex to catch the updraft their feathers lock air like down jackets that s why they don t freeze you ever wonder how they stay warm at 10 000 feet that s a tuck in at the end

latest.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Rocky Mountains have a slow-motion heartbeat
latest.script_key: they re still rising right now but the sky keeps stealing them look close at these layered cliffs you re seeing rock that used to lie at the bottom of ancient seas wind and water strip off half a millimeter every year that s why the peaks stay sharp even while the range shrinks you can watch it happen if you blink slowly enough what s the oldest thing you ve ever stood next to

latest.title: fast rivers move this way and why
latest.script_key: watch the water pick its path see how the current hugs the outside bend that s where the river cuts fastest deepest water moves slower so the fast flow piles up there shaving the bank away that s why rivers snake instead of going straight next time you see a curve you know the water s carving it

latest.title: Forests make cooler air under the canopy
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Horses sleep standing without falling over
latest.script_key: horses can lock their legs while resting watch the legs when the body looks calm because horses have a stay apparatus that helps support them while they rest it lets them doze and react quickly if danger appears the nap is built for speed would you sleep like that

latest.title: Snakes can sense heat when light disappears
latest.script_key: snakes can sense heat when light disappears watch the head before the strike because some snakes have heat sensitive pits that detect warm bodies that gives them a second kind of vision at night the target glows to them without glowing to us which reptile sense next

latest.title: Cows remember faces across the herd
latest.script_key: cows can remember who belongs in the herd watch the calm face first because cows can recognize familiar herd members and people that memory lowers stress when the group feels predictable a quiet look can carry more history than it seems which farm animal should we explain next
